task_completion.description: Navigate to the Surgery module in GNU Health.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GNU Health client is open and the 'Surgery' or 'Surgeries' view is visible.

task_completion.description: Select the patient Ana Betz for the new surgery record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new surgery form is open with 'Ana Betz' entered or selected in the Patient field.
The text 'Laparoscopic Appendectomy' (or containing 'appendectomy') is visible in the Description field of the surgery record.
The Date field in the surgery record shows '2025-01-15' (or '01/15/2025' depending on locale).

task_completion.description: Set the surgery classification to Urgent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Classification field or dropdown in the surgery record is set to 'Urgent'.

task_completion.description: Enter the extra info/notes regarding the procedure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Extra Info or Notes text area contains the required details, specifically mentioning 'appendicitis' and 'laparoscopic'.
The record appears saved, indicated by a generated record ID, a 'Saved' status message, or the save icon being in a disabled/saved state.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the GNU Health desktop client being clicked and typed into, rather than terminal commands executing database queries.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual GNU Health application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard Tryton/GNU Health client, not a text editor or a mock application.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply print the required details to a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The required information is entered into the correct form fields within the GNU Health application, not just echoed in a console.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient: Ana Betz. Expected description keyword: 'appendectomy'. Expected date: '2025-01-15'. Expected classification: 'Urgent' (or 'u'). Expected notes keywords: 'appendicitis', 'laparoscopic'.
